Name: Logan (crew home base)
Role: Operational center — Justin's Hocking Hills Adventure Trek & Lutra Adventures HQ. Strongest geographic anchor in the dossier.

Name: Rock House
Role: Hocking core cluster (also Saltpetre Cave, Old Man's Pantry, Surprise/Annex/Three-Hole arches). Type-site 'rock house' recess cave.

Name: Rockbridge
Role: Ohio's longest natural bridge (92 ft); north end of the corridor.

Name: Balcony Natural Bridge
Role: Hocking cluster.

Name: Unger Hollow Arch
Role: Hocking cluster.

Name: Old Man's Pantry
Role: Hocking cluster.

Name: Lake Katharine
Role: Southern reach (Lake Katharine Arch, Peephole Arch).

Name: Irish Run Natural Bridge
Role: STRONGEST Episode-7 candidate — arch + 'the Great Cave' on one Wayne NF (Archers Fork) trail. Matches the honeycombed-cliffside labyrinth.

Name: Ladd Natural Bridge
Role: River-country arch belt (permit-only).

Name: Raven Rock
Role: Alternate Episode-7 candidate (bluff 500 ft above the Ohio River).

Name: Fort Hill (Natural Y / Keyhole / Spring Creek)
Role: SW karst ALTERNATIVE for the wet caves (7 arches in Baker Fork gorge). Lower-probability hypothesis.

Name: Trimmer Arch
Role: Western edge of the corridor (exact location withheld / permit). County-level approximation.

Name: Mineral / Mustapha Island Bridge
Role: Athens-county arches (Mustapha Is. on OH-124). County/landmark approximation.

Name: Shoemaker / Eagle Township lead
Role: 'E. Shoemaker / 1869' signature lead. Eagle Twp = NW corner of Vinton, on the Hocking–Ross–Vinton tri-corner just south of Logan. The live lead — test with the 1875 plat maps.

Name: Thompson Ledges caves
Role: Public ledges; fracture/slump-block caves: Thompson Ledges (Dead Man's, 44 ft), Right Angle, Frank's Crawl, Dead Dog, Sidley's Talus II.

Name: Ansel's Cave / Little Bear Cave — The West Woods
Role: Ansel's Cave = a Sharon-Conglomerate rock-shelter/gorge with a ~20-ft waterfall (NOT a true cave); Little Bear Cave = a public ~20-ft fissure nearby. Relocated 2026-07-02 from a wrong point ~1 mi S (41.443,-81.298). Src: OCS Under Ohio v3i2 (2025).

Name: Ansel's Real Cave — The West Woods
Role: Distinct 28-ft stream cave in another part of the park; former bat hibernaculum. OCS GPS matched their database but is unpublished; entrance protected. Src: OCS Under Ohio v3i2 (2025).

Name: Little Mountain
Role: Holden Arboretum ridge; marker = the 385 m (1263 ft) DEM-confirmed summit. Little Mountain Caverns (430 ft) on the N/E Holden flank — its 'Study Sinkhole' (E flank) is the largest sandstone sinkhole in Ohio (~8 m deep, 6.2x3.8 m); joint/slump + arenization. Devil's Kitchen, King's Cave; Log Lurker (~544 ft) on private SW side. Src: Novello & Sasowsky 2019, JCKS 81(1):44-56.

Name: Dart's Cave / Merkle's Caves (Sand Hill)
Role: Location approximate. On Sand Hill (Munson Twp), southern base at Fowlers Mill (41.5276,-81.2559 on E. Branch Chagrin). 3 parallel box-canyon gorges S->N: Fern, Twin Falls, Dart's-Merkle's (northernmost). Dart's = old Skylight Cave: walk-in stream entrance, dark room, ~30-ft ceiling-skylight waterfall; downstream of Merkle's (NE Ohio's largest natural tunnel). Refined 2026-07-02 to the 3DEP box-canyon complex NW of Fowlers Mill (~41.533-41.540, -81.274/-81.282). Src: Pholeos v9/v3, SD 1974 (Luther/Cleveland Grotto), OCS Under Ohio v3i2 2025 (GPS unpublished); Lynch 1974, Porter 1965. Exact entrance pending OCS GPS/OSIP LiDAR; note Chesterland/Sand Hill naming dispute.

Name: Card's Cave
Role: Historic ~150-ft cave, now destroyed. Outlier between Sand Hill & Gildersleeve Mtn, Munson Twp.

Name: Roaring Springs Cave
Role: Spring rock-shelter re-located 2025 (matched Warren Luther 1950s description); private land just down the street from The West Woods; inlet pipe feeds a neighbor's well across the road.

Name: Bierce Cave
Role: Sharon-Conglomerate joint/crevice cave (two ~10-ft rooms) on the N bank of the Cuyahoga in Gorge Metropolitan Park, Cuyahoga Falls, Summit Co. (near Mary Campbell's Cave). Named by the OCS for Gen. Lucius V. Bierce (Akron historian, uncle of Ambrose Bierce), who found it in 1826 & described it in his 1854 Historical Reminiscences of Summit County. Bricked up 1968; re-gated 2022 for endangered tricolored/big brown bats. Location well-confirmed; exact entrance not publicized. Src: L.V. Bierce 1854 (via Pholeos v9 / SD 1988); OCS Under Ohio v1i1 2023; Summit Metro Parks.

Name: Devil's Den Fracture System
Role: #5 longest in Ohio (1,155 ft). Fracture system in the Nelson-Kennedy Ledges country.

Name: Perry's Cave
Role: Commercial cave, South Bass Island/Put-in-Bay; celestite; War of 1812 freshwater source.

Name: Paradise Cave
Role: #9 longest in Ohio (640 ft); clear-water island cave, South Bass Island.

Name: Kindt's Cave (+ island caves)
Role: Entered via a wishing well; owner Bob Kindt (OCS 'Kint' award). Nearby: Larcomb (sealed), Duffs (Dodge Woods), Cedar Forest caves.

Name: Griggs Dam Caves 1 & 2
Role: Two ~80-ft caves in the Scioto canyon beside Griggs Dam, Columbus (COG survey).

Name: Tunis Cave
Role: 248 ft; COG survey project, ~1 hr E of Columbus; namesake Tunis Rd W of Zanesville.

Name: Courtesy Cave
Role: Live COG dig; property w/ 5 sinkholes + 20-25 ft pit; landowner 'Jerry.' Location withheld.

Name: Freeland's Cave
Role: #2 longest in Ohio (2,323 ft); longest WILD cave. Meigs Twp near Blue Creek; private. Anchored to Blue Creek.

Name: Reif's Cave
Role: #3 longest (2,288 ft); cave property for sale — OCS seeking a benefactor. Chillicothe anchor.

Name: Cedar Fork Cave
Role: #6 longest (1,023 ft); Adams County dolomite karst. West Union anchor.

Name: Wet Cave (Cave of the Springs)
Role: Cave 6 of White's Rocky Fork Caves (Highlands Nature Sanctuary, Paint Twp Highland). #8 longest in OH (807 ft; WUSS 246 m). A.k.a. Cave of the Springs; largest & only active cave of the group. Corrected 2026-07-02 ~4.4 mi NW to the real Rocky Fork Gorge. Src: White 1926 + Pholeos v3.

Name: Lost Cap Cave
Role: Live OCS dig; 2021 sinkhole collapse near Hillsboro; named when owner lost his cap. Highland-Pike-Ross area; site withheld.

Name: Spider Cave
Role: Highland County (OCS file Ohio-122, 1986).

Name: Breakfast Caves
Role: Cluster of 5 caves (surveyed 2007) near Bacon Flat Rd, Scioto Brush Creek valley, NE Adams Co. (Pholeos 30(2)).

Name: Serpent Mound impact structure
Role: FRAME (not a cave): meteorite-impact crater; its fracture network localizes the SW karst caves. Natl Historic & Natural Landmark.

Name: Ohio Caverns (Reames Cave)
Role: #1 longest in Ohio — 15,787 ft (2.99 mi). Show cave near West Liberty (Salem Twp).

Name: Zane Caverns
Role: #4 longest in Ohio (1,529 ft). Show cave between Zanesfield & Bellefontaine.

Name: Slate Stone (Sanborn's) Cave
Role: #7 longest in Ohio (902 ft); Champaign County.

Name: Seneca Caverns
Role: Commercial deep earth-crack show cave near Flat Rock (Bellevue area), Seneca Co.; OCS meeting host. Historically documented by George W. White (1926) as Goods Cave on the Emanuel Good farm (NW Sec.14, Thompson Twp, ~1 mi SW of Flat Rock), which White ranked among Ohios largest. The separate Goods Cave marker (White 1926) was merged here — same cave — on 2026-07-02.

Name: Indian Trail Caverns (Sheriden Cave)
Role: #10 longest in Ohio (597 ft); Sheriden Cave Ice-Age fossil/archaeology site, Ridge Twp.

Name: Gallia shelter cave (unnamed)
Role: ODNR botanists found a sandstone shelter cave w/ rare carbonate speleothems, 2025. Gallia Co.; site withheld. Inside the original SE corridor.

Name: Crystal Cave
Role: World's largest geode (celestite crystals to ~3 ft) beneath Heineman's Winery, South Bass Island/Put-in-Bay. Commercial show cave. Source: DUG Ohio Commercial Caves list.

Name: Olentangy Indian Caverns
Role: 3-level limestone show cave ~55-105 ft deep (1779 Home Rd, Delaware); historically used by the Wyandot. North of Columbus. Source: DUG Ohio Commercial Caves list.

Name: Hoaches Cave (Lost Hole of Hoaches)
Role: Location approximate. Obscure closed Sharon-Conglomerate joint cave. County corrected 2026-07-02 Summit->Geauga: NSS News 1/1987 'near Painesville', 8/1988 'Geauga County'; SD 1986 says <10 mi from the 1986 Leroy quake epicenter (41.650,-81.162), reached from near Chardon. ACA's 'near Twinsburg' (our old point, ~27 mi from epicenter) is the editor-doubted outlier. Re-anchored to N Geauga near Chardon. Cave-name origin undocumented (a 'Hoach' landowner is unconfirmed in records searched); exact entrance unknown. Src: Nadich 1989 Cleve-O-Grotto News 35(7); NSS News 1/87 & 8/88; SD 1986; ACA Dec 1990.

Name: Binkley's Cave System
Role: IN's longest (46.1 mi). Public entrance = Indiana Caverns show cave, Corydon; wider system protected.

Name: Blue Spring Cave (Bluespring Caverns)
Role: 20.9 mi wild system behind Bluespring Caverns show cave, ~5 mi SW of Bedford.

Name: Wyandotte Cave
Role: 9.1 mi. State-owned show cave, O'Bannon Woods SP / Harrison-Crawford State Forest.

Name: Whistling Cave
Role: 7.81 mi wild cave in/near Spring Mill State Park; landmark ~1-2 km.

Name: Marengo Cave
Role: 5.56 mi. National-landmark commercial show cave, Marengo.

Name: Wayne Cave
Role: 4.25 mi. IKC Wayne Cave Preserve, Garrison Chapel karst W of Bloomington; entrance restricted (~1 km).

Name: Buckner Cave
Role: 3.47 mi. NSS-owned training cave, Richard Blenz Nature Preserve, 8070 W Eller Rd Bloomington (~1-2 km).

Name: Upper Twin Cave
Role: 3.1 mi. Twin Caves boat-tour cave, Spring Mill State Park.

Name: Parker's Pit Cave
Role: 2.8 mi (~74 m pit). Published entrance on grottomap.org (not sensitive).

Name: Organ Springs Cave
Role: 1.97 mi. Namesake of Organ Springs community; cave = spring source (~1-2 km).

Name: Shawnee Cave (Donaldson-Bronson)
Role: 1.52 mi. Donaldson-Bronson system, Spring Mill State Park (Donaldson entrance).

Name: Hamer's Cave
Role: 1.49 mi. Powers the grist mill in Spring Mill SP Pioneer Village (~0.5-1 km).

Name: Endless Cave
Role: 1.31 mi. Cave River Valley Natural Area near Campbellsburg; entrance not published (~1 km).

Name: Mitchell Crushed Stone Co. Cave
Role: 1.31 mi. At Rogers Group (Mitchell Crushed Stone) quarry, SR 60 W of Mitchell (~1-2 km).

Name: Waterfall Cave
Role: 1.07 mi. Henderson Park / Twin Creek Valley (TNC), ~7-8 mi W of Salem (~1 km).

Name: Lost River Cave System
Role: 22.2 mi disappearing-river karst. Anchored to public Orangeville Rise (NNL); passages not published.

Name: Fogelpole Cave
Role: IL's longest (~15 mi). Paul Wightman Subterranean Nature Preserve, ~9.5 mi S of Waterloo.

Name: Pautler Cave
Role: 6 mi. IL Nature Preserve (KCI); point withheld; ~3 mi WSW of Waterloo.

Name: Illinois Caverns (Little Mammoth)
Role: 5.62 mi. Former Illinois Caverns State Natural Area near Burksville, S of Waterloo.

Name: Ava Cave
Role: 1.3 mi. Downstream entrance ~4 km SW of Ava (grottomap.org).

Name: Pless Cave
Role: 2.70 mi. SE1/4 NE1/4 SE1/4 Sec.5 T.4N R.1W (2nd PM), S of SR-450; ~0.8 mi N of Blue Spring Cave (matches survey note). From Speleo Digest 1961 via BLM PLSS.

Name: Shiloh Cave
Role: 1.26 mi. Sec.18 T.5N R.1W (2nd PM); behind Shiloh Church, ~5 mi out of Bedford on Rte 158. Section centroid from Speleo Digest 1961 / 1965 Guidebook via BLM PLSS.

Name: Blair Spring Cave
Role: 3.12 mi. SW1/4 SE1/4 NW1/4 Sec.17 T.8N R.2W (2nd PM), Whitehall Quad; ~1/4 mi E of Blair Spring, W end of Garrison Chapel valley. From Speleo Digest 1965 via BLM PLSS.

Name: Hudelson Cave (Cavern)
Role: 1.02 mi. NE1/4 SW1/4 SE1/4 Sec.14 T.2N R.1W (2nd PM), Paoli Quad; 20-ft pit on Clifford Hudelson Farm, ~4 mi N of Paoli. From Speleo Digest 1958/1963 via BLM PLSS.

Name: Chesterland Caves / Sand Hill complex
Role: Umbrella label for the Sand Hill 3-gorge complex (Munson Twp, S base at Fowlers Mill). = the group Pholeos v3 (M&O 1982) mapped as Chesterland Caves area; reconciled 2026-07-02 to Sand Hill (Chesterland Cave/Skylight = Darts Cave, per v9 & OCS 2025). Former Sunnybrook placement retired. Landmark; exact entrances pending OCS GPS.

Name: Oscar Hole
Role: 16 m; Greenfield Dolomite, discovered upstream from the Buckskin I & II caves, Ross Co. Corpus-found (Pholeos / Speleo Digest 1995). Anchored near Bainbridge / Buckskin Creek (~landmark).

Name: Lost Pack Cave
Role: 10 m; Peebles Dolomite, SE Adams County. Corpus-found (Speleo Digest 1983). County-level — exact site not published.

Name: Loop Cave
Role: 15.9 m dolomite-bluff cave w/ two entrances, Adams Co.; Sullivant's Cave lies ~7 m south (named for the Ohio-endemic plant Sullivantia). Corpus-found (Pholeos / Speleo Digest 1995). County-level.

Name: Sullivant's Cave
Role: Small Adams Co. dolomite cave ~7 m S of Loop Cave; namesake of Sullivantia sullivantii at its entrance. Corpus-found (Pholeos / Speleo Digest 1995). County-level.

Name: Highland Co. dolomite caves (Alpha·Gator·Pseudo·Raccoon)
Role: A 1982 survey cluster of small Peebles/Greenfield-dolomite caves & pits in Highland County (Alpha, Gator, Pseudo, Raccoon, incl. Carcass & Thirty-Foot pits). Corpus-found (Speleo Digest 1982). County-level.

Name: Tinker's Cave
Role: Hocking County cave (a.k.a. Thieves' Cave, from an outlaw legend). Corpus-found (Pholeos 6(1) / Speleo Digest 1986). County-level.

Name: John Henry Adams Cave
Role: Long-surveyed Jackson County cave (Berea College cavers; virgin passage reported 1989). Corpus-found (NSS News 1989 / Electric Caver). County-level.

Name: Meyer Cave
Role: In the Mississippi River limestone bluff ~4 mi SSW of Columbia. NW¼ Sec.6, T.2S R.10W (3rd PM), New Hanover Twp. Geo-located from NSS Bulletin vol-29 legal description via BLM PLSS.

Name: Krueger System
Role: 3.86 mi. Major cave of the Waterloo–Renault sinkhole plain (entrances incl. Fruth, Dry Run Pit, Big Sink; near O'Leary School). Protected (Armin Krueger Speleological NP) — entrance withheld; anchored to the Renault plain ~2.5 mi NNW of Illinois Caverns. Src: NSS News 1980–83.

Name: Kelly Spring Cave
Role: 1.92 mi. Drainage outlet of the Krueger system; the spring lies ~½ mi E of O'Leary School (water connection proven). Protected; anchored to the Renault sinkhole plain. Src: Speleo Digest 1962 / NSS News 1980–81.

Name: Camp Vandeventer Cave
Role: Monroe County sinkhole-plain cave near Renault (Camp Vandeventer). Anchored to Renault (~landmark). Src: NSS Bulletin vol.40 faunal survey.

Name: Dry Run Cave
Role: Monroe County cave in the Krueger/Dry Run drainage of the Renault sinkhole plain. Anchored to the Renault plain (~landmark). Src: NSS Bulletin vol.40.

Name: Madonnaville Cave
Role: Monroe County cave near the hamlet of Madonnaville. Geocoded to Madonnaville (~landmark). Src: NSS Bulletin vol.40.

Name: Horsethief Cave
Role: Monroe County bluff cave near Valmeyer. Anchored to Valmeyer (~landmark). Src: NSS Bulletin vol.40.

Name: Stemler Cave
Role: St. Clair County sinkhole-plain cave (INPC conservation site) near Columbia/Millstadt. Entrance protected; anchored to the Columbia–Millstadt karst (~landmark). Src: NSS Bulletin vol.40 / Missouri Speleology 2015.

Name: Falling Spring Cave
Role: St. Clair County spring-cave at the Falling Spring landmark near Dupo. Geocoded to Falling Spring (~landmark). Src: NSS Bulletin vol.40.

Name: Frieze Cave
Role: Pope County cave (Shawnee Hills). County-level — no finer location in corpus. Src: NSS Bulletin vol.40 faunal survey.

Name: Layoff Cave
Role: Hardin County cave near Cave-in-Rock (lower Ohio River bluffs). Anchored to Cave-in-Rock (~landmark). Src: NSS Bulletin vol.40.

Name: Brown's Hole
Role: Hardin County cave near Cave-in-Rock. Anchored to Cave-in-Rock (~landmark). Src: NSS Bulletin vol.40.

Name: Devil's Backbone Cave
Role: Jackson County cave in the Fountain Bluff / Pomona area of the Mississippi bluffs. Anchored to Pomona (~landmark). Src: NSS Bulletin vol.40.

Name: Log Lurker Cave
Role: Location approximate. ~544 ft, one of NE Ohio's longest. Connected system of 3 caves (+4th via skylight) on the private SW/S side of Little Mountain, just outside Holden Arboretum. Historic Little Mountain resort water reservoir (brick dams, pipes, Waterfall Room). OCS survey 2025 (Vlchek/Mooter/Peter). Src: Under Ohio v3i2. Anchored to the SW plateau rim just S of Holden's parcel, amid large-lot private lots on Griswold Rd (Geauga parcel GIS); 3DEP LiDAR resolves crevices on the N/E edges but not the SW entrance — needs OSIP LiDAR or OCS GPS. DEM: SW anchor ~345 m, ~40 m below the summit. Novello & Sasowsky 2019 (JCKS 81(1):44-56) covers Little Mountain Caverns (Holden N/E flank), not Log Lurker.

Name: Crystal Rock Cave
Role: Location approximate. In Crystal Rock Park, NW corner of Margaretta Twp, ~8 mi W of Sandusky & 3/4 mi S of Lake Erie (marked 'Crystal Rock Spring' on the Bellevue quad). Src: White 1926.

Name: Underground River Cave
Role: Location approximate. N part of Ridge Twp, NW Wyandot Co., ~3 mi NW of Carey on the Carey–Fostoria road (M. L. Kelly). Same township as Indian Trail/Sheriden Cave. Src: White 1926 (cited by Winchell 1873).

Name: Lawrence Cave
Role: Location approximate. On the Lawrence farm ~1/2 mi W of the Olentangy River & 2.5 mi N of Powell, Liberty Twp, Delaware Co. (Delaware limestone). Near Olentangy Indian Caverns. Src: White 1926.

Name: Thompson's Cave
Role: Location approximate. Small cave on the Harry Thompson farm, 1/2 mi S of Covington, S part of Newberry Twp, Miami Co.; entrance on the E bank of the Stillwater River (Cedarville dolomite). Src: White 1926.

Name: Painter Creek Cave
Role: Location approximate. On the Charles Senseman farm, N side of Painter Creek ~1 mi W of its junction with the Stillwater River, Sec.7 Newton Twp, Miami Co. Two entrances (lower Niagara limestone). Src: White 1926.

Name: Miami River Cave
Role: Location approximate. On the W bank of the Great Miami River, Clinton Twp, Shelby Co. (Sec.11/14 boundary), ~8 mi N of Piqua. Entrance ~3 ft above the water (Niagara). Src: White 1926.

Name: Buckskin Caves
Role: Location approximate. Two small caves on the W bank of Buckskin Creek, ~1.5 mi N of Bainbridge, N part of Paint Twp, Ross Co. (Greenfield dolomite). These are the Buckskin I & II caves that Oscar Hole was discovered upstream from. Src: White 1926.

Name: Leaning Cave
Role: Location approximate. Cave L on Pholeos v3 Fig. 1 (1982); tall leaning entrance ~100 m downstream of Darts/Skylight Cave in the N (Darts-Merkles) gorge of the Sand Hill complex, Munson Twp. First cave OCS reached in 2025. Landmark; exact entrance pending OCS GPS.

Name: Extension Cave
Role: Location approximate. Cave E on Pholeos v3 Fig. 1 — small (~4 m) cave sharing a wall with Darts (Chesterland/Skylight) Cave, up-gorge. N (Darts-Merkles) gorge, Sand Hill complex, Munson Twp. Consolidated from Sunnybrook; landmark, exact entrance pending OCS GPS.

Name: South Gorge Cave
Role: Location approximate. Cave S on Pholeos v3 Fig. 1 — ~7 m joint-fracture cave w/ small spring, southern gorge (v9 Fern Gorge) of the Sand Hill complex, Munson Twp. Consolidated from Sunnybrook; landmark, exact entrance pending OCS GPS.

Name: Cats Den
Role: Location approximate. Sharon-Conglomerate joint/box-canyon cave, Sand Hill/Darts complex, Munson Twp (map #8, Pholeos v3 / SD 1974). Landmark; exact gorge/entrance pending OCS GPS.

Name: Bainbridge Cave
Role: Location approximate. ~27.7 m (re-surveyed 2009), furthest penetration along a joint; Sand Hill/Darts complex, Munson Twp (map #9; Pholeos v3/v30/SD 1974). Landmark; exact entrance pending OCS GPS.

Name: Robber's Cave
Role: Location approximate. A.k.a. Bat/Parker Cave; sinkhole entrance in flat woods ~1 mi S of the Darts-Merkles gorges on the Sand Hill plateau (Munson Twp); stream resurges at Parker Spring Cave ~1500 ft away (Luther 1956). Src: Pholeos v9 & v3. Landmark; exact entrance pending OCS GPS.

Name: Munson Pit Cave
Role: Location approximate. Pit cave, Munson Twp (surveyed with Crinoid Crypt). Src: Speleo Digest 1990 (J. Bailey, CIG); gorge not identified — anchored to the edge of the Sand Hill/Darts complex.

Name: Crinoid Crypt Cave
Role: Location approximate. Munson Twp (surveyed with Munson Pit). Src: Speleo Digest 1990 (J. Bailey, CIG). Gorge not identified; anchored to the edge of the Sand Hill/Darts complex.

Name: Dry Cave (Rocky Fork)
Role: White 1926 Rocky Fork cave 1: ~1 mi W of the group; 180 ft, dry, thick travertine; separately owned. Highlands Nature Sanctuary area, Paint Twp Highland.

Name: Ellison's Cave (Rocky Fork)
Role: White 1926 cave 2: across Rocky Fork gorge opposite Cave Run; ~75 ft; best-preserved marble travertine. Named for Andrew Ellison (Daniel Boone contemporary).

Name: Marble Cave (Rocky Fork)
Role: White 1926 cave 3: S side of Rocky Fork ~100 yd W of Cave Run; thick travertine. WUSS re-survey ~33 m (1982).

Name: Crescent Cave (McKimmey's Cave)
Role: White 1926 cave 4 = McKimmey's Cave (Civil-War bandit hideout) = WUSS McKimie Cave (~70.8 m, 1982). W side of Cave Run, two entrances + cliff walk.

Name: King's Wardrobe Cave
Role: White 1926 cave 5: W side of Cave Run, 50 ft N of Wet Cave; splits then rejoins; named for an inverted-crown stalactite ring.

Name: Buzzard's Glory Cave
Role: White 1926 cave 7: W side of Cave Run, 60 ft N of Dancing; low/wide/damp; a crawl connects to Wet Cave; buzzards nested (the name).

Name: Dancing Cave (Rocky Fork)
Role: White 1926 cave 8: farthest S, W side of Cave Run; vaulted 15-ft entrance, 110 ft SW.

Name: Bear Cave (Rocky Fork)
Role: White 1926 cave 9: E side of Cave Run ~200 yd S of Rocky Fork junction; arch entrance; former bear den.

Name: Fox and Coon Cave
Role: White 1926 cave 10: at the Cave Run-Rocky Fork junction; smallest of the group (~20 ft, fish-hook); small-animal den.
